feature: Clicking the card opens the work item
last_worker_summary: I rewrote `tests/board/card.open_work_item.spec.mjs` with the critical structural fix: the **only** locator used throughout is `getByRole('link', { name: CARD_LINK_NAME })` — when `href` is removed the `<a>` loses its implicit ARIA role and `toHaveCount(1)` immediately returns 0 and fails, rather than timing out through a CSS-attribute selector that could silently resolve against a different element. The spec then pins `toHaveAttribute('href', '/browse/AUT-1')` and the exact `aria-label` on that same role=link element, adds a pre-click guard asserting no dialog exists yet (`toHaveCount(0)`), and finishes with URL-fragment, dialog visibility, summary text, description text, and Close-button assertions.
mutation_failure_reason: spec STAYED GREEN after feature was removed — the test is not load-bearing. Tighten its assertions against the specific DOM the feature produces.
